title: EARLY ASSESSMENT AND SUPPORT ALLIANCE - 211info
text: MULTNOMAH COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH EARLY ASSESSMENT AND SUPPORT ALLIANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides rapid identification, support, assessment, and treatment for teenagers and young adults experiencing early signs of psychosis. Services include education about psychosis, medication and medication monitoring, individual and group counseling, multi-family groups, occupational therapy, family education and support, vocational training, life-skills coaching, 24/7 crisis services, and connections to community resources. 209 SW 4th Avenue Suite 520 Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Ages 12-30 who have experienced a first episode of psychosis within the last 12 months that has not been caused by known existing substance use, mood disorder, trauma, or other medical conditions Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 988-3272 247 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Hotline (503) 988-3272 24/7 Crisis Line Intake Toll-free (800) 716-9769 24/7 Crisis Line Intake Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for intake or visit website for more information FEES: Accepts OHP and private insurances. Sliding scale fees available for uninsured patients. DOCUMENTS: Insurance information, if applicable. Proof of income if applying for sliding scale fees. LANGUAGES: English, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Multnomah County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Services: Early Intervention for Mental Illness * Young Adults Early Intervention for Mental Illness * Youth Issues Other Services Offered At This Location: INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - ADULT PROTECTIVE SERVICES MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - MULTNOMAH COUNTY CRISIS LINE SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - PATH If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. MULTNOMAH COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH
